results.title: 8 Best Google Analytics Alternatives
results.content:
KEY METRICS TRACKED: Pageviews, unique visitors, bounce rate, visit duration, traffic sources, top pages, goal conversions, campaign performance

PRICING: Starts at $9/month based on pageviews

FREE PLAN / TRIAL: 30-day free trial; no free plan

Plausible is a simple, privacy-focused Google Analytics alternative. It doesn’t use any cookies, so you know it’s compliant with all the different privacy laws.

  Plausible Analytics - Google Analytics alternative  

The drawback with Plausible is that it really is very simplistic. You’re not going to get any advanced analytics here. However, it does track goals, conversions, and campaigns, so you still get those basic metrics that you need to draw conclusions about how people are using your site. [...] Self-hosted version requires technical expertise to maintain
 Interface isn’t as polished as some modern alternatives
 Can be resource-intensive on your server
 Support is limited on the free self-hosted version
 Initial setup is more complex than plug-and-play alternatives

Get started with Matomo.

### 3. Heap

 Heap - Google Analytics Alternative 

BEST FOR: Growing companies and startups that need insights without extensive setup time

KEY METRICS TRACKED: User events, session replays, conversion funnels, retention rates, user properties, behavioral paths, feature adoption

PRICING: Free up to 10,000 sessions/month; paid plans require contacting sales

FREE PLAN / TRIAL: Free plan for up to 10,000 sessions; trial available for higher tiers [...] KEY METRICS TRACKED: Pageviews, sessions, bounce rate, conversions, eCommerce transactions, goal completions, heatmap clicks, A/B test results

PRICING: Cloud hosting starts at $23/month; self-hosted version is free

FREE PLAN / TRIAL: Free self-hosted version available; cloud version has free trial

Matomo is a Google Analytics alternative that’s focused on privacy protection and data ownership, in order to make sure you’re GDPR compliant. With 100% data ownership, you get the power to protect your users’ privacy. You know where your data is stored and what’s happening to it, without external influence.

  Matomo Dashboard - Google Analytics alternatives  

Matomo is a pretty full-featured alternative to Google Analytics. It has many of the same reports, plus heatmaps and A/B testing.

results.title: 4 Open Source (FREE) Alternatives to Google Analytics
results.content:
# 4 Open Source (FREE) Alternatives to Google Analytics
## Jeff Sauer - Service Stacking
44500 subscribers
47 likes

### Description
3708 views
Posted: 28 Jun 2022
🎯 Want a complete set of our review and recommendations on google analytics alternatives? ▶️ Visit  

In this video, I’m going to share with you four free and open-source alternatives to Google Analytics. But are they really free and are they worth trying out? That’s the catch! While open-source software is free to use, someone needs to pay to store the data. That makes the software a little more technical in nature and harder to use.

0:00 - Intro
0:50 - #4 Open Web Analytics
2:39 - #3 AWStats
5:12 - #2 Plausible
7:02 - #1 Snowplow Analytics
8:19 - Conclusion

results.title: 10 Best Google Analytics Alternatives for 2026 (Compared)
results.content:
Umami Analytics dashboard

Umami Analytics dashboard.

It's lightweight, privacy-friendly, and runs smoothly on modest hardware. Many teams switched from Plausible to Umami because it demands fewer server resources.

Highlights:

 Free and open-source: self-host on GitHub
 Cookieless tracking, GDPR-compliant
 Lightweight footprint that runs on small VPS instances
 Less likely to be blocked by ad blockers than GA4
 Custom events and basic reporting

Best for: Developers who want free, self-hosted analytics with minimal overhead.

Limitations: No funnels, revenue tracking, or advanced segmentation. Community-driven support only. UI is functional but less polished than paid alternatives.

## 7. Pirsch Analytics [...] On speed and design, I cared about both. The dashboard is the fastest in this list: it loads instantly, feels snappy when you filter or switch views, and stays out of your way. It's also modern and clean, built recently from scratch, not a legacy interface patched over the years. Plausible and Fathom look fine, but they feel older. Seline is the one I'd actually want to open every day.

The extra features are there when you want them, not forced on you:

Seline analytics dashboard

Seline's dashboard: modern, clean, and the fastest to use in this list.

Optional, when you need more: [...] The interface is dense. Setup takes longer than it should. Privacy rules keep shifting. Cookie banners frustrate visitors. And answering a simple question like "which page drove the most sign-ups last week?" can mean clicking through five reports.

This is why privacy-focused tools are becoming increasingly popular. Options like Plausible, Fathom, Simple Analytics, and Seline (which I developed) eliminate the unnecessary complexity, avoid invasive tracking, and provide the essential metrics you need without a lengthy learning curve.

In this guide, I compare the best Google Analytics alternatives for 2026. I'll start with Seline, then walk through the other tools I'd recommend and when each one makes sense.

### TL;DR: Best Google Analytics Alternatives

results.title: What alternative to Google Analytics you using now and ...
results.content:
# What alternative to Google Analytics you using now and why?

## Post details
r/webdev | 79 upvotes | 97 comments | Posted by u/stevephuc on 2024-08-15T13:12:47.231Z

## Content
On July 1st, Google will delete ALL your old Google Analytics data You can NOT move it to the new Google Analytics 4 (GA4) Any free website analytics that is not Google Analytics? Which one you using now (especially not that complex?) UPDATE: thank all for your input. According to alteropen.com , european-alternatives.eu and from all: Matomo, Plausible, PostHog, Umami, Fathom seems to be the most popular alternative [...] - Metrix Analytics , utvecklad och driftsatt i Sverige 🙂
- Rybbit is one of the most modern ones, and is open source + GDPR compliant
- Checkout plainsignal.com , a lightweight, privacy-focused, cookie-free ga alternative with built-in webvital that can help you to understand the source of your leads, pageviews and slow loading pages(with built-in web vitals) in a single page report.x
- I made a comparison of Google Analytics 4 vs Umami vs Plausible after using them for 30+ days. Check it out as a post:  ... or as a YouTube video:  TLDR: Umami wins on price, data quality, data access, ease of use and unique features. This post was NOT sponsored, and I am not affiliated with Umami in any way.
- Here is a 60+ list: [...] - I've tried most of the main ones and ended up building my own, so I'll be honest about all of them: Plausible ($9/mo) — the polished default. Single page, no learning curve. Best choice if you want a cleaner GA4 and don't care about dashboards being your primary interface. Open source, EU-based. Fathom ($15/mo) — same idea as Plausible but with multi-site pricing that doesn't punish traffic spikes. Great for agencies and people running many sites. Simple Analytics ($15/mo) — strongest privacy story, has public dashboards if you want to share your numbers transparently. Umami (free, self-hosted) — runs on Vercel in 10 minutes. Best free option if you don't mind running it yourself. Muro ($5/mo,) — different angle from the rest. Instead of a dashboard, Muro emails you a 2-minute summary
